patches.summary: Resolve {email}/{token}/{pp_contact_email}/{COMPANY_PP_CONTACT_EMAIL} placeholders in AuthHeader to the configured COMPANY_PP_CONTACT_EMAIL value.
patches.reason: Generator emitted the literal placeholder when the format string used aliases other than {token}; SEC fair-access requires a real contact email in User-Agent or every request 403s.

patches.summary: Drop explicit Accept-Encoding header so net/http auto-decodes gzip responses.
patches.reason: Setting Accept-Encoding manually disables Go's transparent gzip decoding, leaving callers with raw compressed bytes (first surfaced on company_tickers.json).

patches.summary: When primaryDocument points at an XSL HTML wrapper, fall back to the accession's index.json to locate the actual Form 4 XML.
patches.reason: SEC's submissions index increasingly returns wrapper paths (xslF345X05/wf-form4_*.xml) instead of the raw XML; without the fallback, every modern Form 4 ingests as a parse failure.

patches.summary: Pre-strip HTML tags before running the ITEM-header regex in extractSections.
patches.reason: SEC 10-K bodies embed ITEM headers inside font/span/b tags; without the strip pass the boundary regex matches nothing and every sections call returns boundary_unverifiable.

patches.summary: When multiple substantial candidate boundaries exist for the same Item, prefer the latest candidate iff it is >3x larger than every earlier substantial candidate; otherwise return boundary_unverifiable.
patches.reason: Modern SEC 10-Ks ship a TOC entry for Item X followed by the body for Item X; the v1 parser couldn't tell them apart and bailed. v1.1 heuristic preserves boundary safety while recovering the common case.

patches.summary: Pick the chronologically earliest material 8-K after each insider sale, not the most recent.
patches.reason: ListEdgarFilings returns rows ORDER BY filed_at DESC; the original forward-iterate-and-break matched the latest material 8-K in the 90-day window, contradicting the command's documented semantics. Loop now iterates ascending via a new findEarliestMaterial8K helper covered by insider_followthrough_test.go.
patches.validated_outcome: insider_followthrough_test.go::TestFindEarliestMaterial8K_PicksChronologicallyFirst exercises a DESC-sorted set with three material 8-Ks and asserts the earliest is selected.

patches.summary: Route fetchAbsoluteRaw (and therefore fetchSubmissions / refreshTickerCache / fetchFilingBody / fetchForm4XML) through a new client.DoRaw method that wraps the AdaptiveLimiter.
patches.reason: fetchAbsoluteRaw previously called c.HTTPClient.Do directly, bypassing the limiter that c.do() honors. Every hand-rolled SEC fetch ignored fair-access pacing.

patches.summary: Surface UpsertEdgarInsiderTransaction failures in Form4SkipReport instead of discarding them.
patches.reason: The previous `_ = db.UpsertEdgarInsiderTransaction(...)` swallowed write errors so silently-missing transactions had no diagnostic in the skip report — same loud-skip discipline now applies to writes.

patches.summary: Drop redundant second `c.CompanyPpContactEmail == ""` check in AuthHeader (token already aliases the field; earlier guard returned).
patches.reason: Dead-code cleanup flagged by Greptile review.

patches.summary: Remove unreachable `if err == nil { _ = cached }` nested inside `if err != nil` in resolveTickerToCIK.
patches.reason: Dead-code cleanup flagged by Greptile review; the `_ = cached` was suppressing a lint warning that would otherwise surface the dead block. Stale-cache fallback below is preserved.

patches.summary: Gate the fmt.Sprintf table-name interpolation in ListIDs behind a {companies, filings} allowlist; unknown names fall through to the generic resources table.
patches.reason: Defensive fix flagged by Greptile review — interpolating a caller-provided string into a SELECT is a latent SQL-injection surface even though current callers pass our own table names.

patches.summary: Surface explicit truncation when the Form 4 ingest cap clips older filings; expose via --max-form4 flag and form4_truncated + form4_total_in_window JSON fields on insider-summary, insider-followthrough, and primary-sources.
patches.reason: The previous hard `LIMIT 200` in ingestForm4ForCIK silently dropped the oldest 200+ Form 4s for high-volume filers (large biotechs, late-stage offerings) and reported form4_skipped_count: 0 — a false clean-bill. The cap stays as a DB/API pressure rail but is now configurable and reports truncation in-band plus a stderr WARN. Added store.CountEdgarFilings + planForm4Ingest helpers so the truncation decision is independently testable.
patches.validated_outcome: insider_summary_test.go::TestPlanForm4Ingest_TruncatesWhenAboveCap seeds 250 Form 4 filings for a stub CIK and asserts Truncated=true + TotalInWindow=250 under the default --max-form4 200; companion tests cover the clean-bill (50 filings) and cap-disabled (max-form4=0) branches.
